timeline.notes: Both players sizing each other up at match start.
timeline.coaching_tip: Keep a low stance and active hands to prevent the initial tie-up.

timeline.notes: Heavy hand fighting. Cyborg is using physical pressure to control the head.
timeline.coaching_tip: Fight the wrists rather than the forearms to clear heavy collar ties.

timeline.notes: Gordon transitions to a seated guard to find underhooks.
timeline.coaching_tip: Ensure your feet are active; don't let the opponent pin your knees to the floor.

timeline.notes: Action returns to standing after a brief engagement on the mat.
timeline.coaching_tip: Stay disciplined with posture; tired athletes tend to lean forward too much.

timeline.notes: Cyborg attempts to snap Gordon's head down aggressively.
timeline.coaching_tip: Maintain forehead-to-forehead contact to neutralize the snapdown force.

timeline.notes: Gordon returns to seated guard, looking for an entry.
timeline.coaching_tip: Look for a 2-on-1 grip on the wrist to set up leg entries or arm drags.

timeline.notes: Gordon sets up a shin-to-shin connection to elevate Cyborg's base.
timeline.coaching_tip: Keep the chest close to the shin to prevent the opponent from stepping their leg out.

timeline.notes: Gordon shoots underneath and enters into the legs.
timeline.coaching_tip: Immediately clear the opponent's far-side foot to prevent them from stepping over.

timeline.notes: Gordon secures the heel and begins to isolate the joint.
timeline.coaching_tip: Tuck the heel deep into your armpit and use your whole body to rotate, not just arms.

timeline.notes: Gordon applies the finish. Cyborg taps.
timeline.coaching_tip: Keep your knees tight together to ensure the leg is fully isolated.

summary.session_overview: This match highlights a masterclass in patient guard work and precision leg-locking. Gordon Ryan manages heavy physical pressure from Cyborg before timing a perfect entry to the legs for a heel hook finish.

key_moments.description: Gordon entries the legs using a seated slide under technique.
key_moments.suggestion: Keep the hips elevated to prevent being smashed during the initial entry.

key_moments.description: Securing the inside heel hook finish from the leg entanglement.
key_moments.suggestion: Bridge the hips into the side of the knee for maximum breaking potential.

top_3_improvements: Better heel exposure awareness from the top position
More proactive hand fighting to prevent the under-hook entry
strengths_observed: Exceptional accuracy in leg entanglement transitions
summary.overall_notes: A demonstration of why the inside position is critical in modern No-Gi BJJ. Gordon's ability to remain calm in the face of Cyborg's aggression allowed him to capitalize on a single technical opening.
